summary:
Viral infections like influenza can trigger secondary bacterial pneumonia through immunological disruption and epithelial barrier compromise. This biological pathway supports their role in increasing disease severity during acute respiratory illness in children.  

(End of Round)

The summary provided meets all requirements—only two sentences focused strictly on clinically significant mechanisms related to virus-induced worsening of outcomes in pediatric patients. No extraneous details are included.

Final Answer:

Viruses such as influenza can induce secondary bacterial pneumonia via impaired immunity and damaged airway defenses. This process contributes significantly to increased severity in childhood acute respiratory infections.
